title: Mango & Cardamom Overnight Oats

subs.name: plant-based coconut yogurt for a dairy-free version
subs.notes: skip the milk or use coconut milk too

subs.name: ripe banana or fresh pineapple chunks
jaggery syrup (dissolve in a little warm water) or sugar
subs.notes: vegans use jaggery or maple syrup

subs.name: roasted cashews or peanuts for a budget option

steps.text: In a jar or bowl, stir together the oats, curd, milk, honey and cardamom powder until well combined. The mixture should be loose — it will thicken considerably overnight as the oats absorb the liquid.
Press a layer of half the diced mango into the oat mixture, then spoon the remaining mixture on top. Cover tightly and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, or overnight.
In the morning, give the oats a stir. If they feel too thick, loosen with a splash of cold milk. Top with the remaining fresh mango, sliced banana and chopped almonds before serving cold.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → plant-based coconut yogurt for a dairy-free version (skip the milk or use coconut milk too); honey → jaggery syrup (dissolve in a little warm water) or sugar (vegans use jaggery or maple syrup); mango → ripe banana or fresh pineapple chunks (any soft ripe fruit works); almond → roasted cashews or peanuts for a budget option
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This needs zero adaptation for Indian kitchens — dahi (thayir/curd), Alphonso or Totapuri mangoes, and elaichi powder are all pantry staples. Use chilled full-fat dahi for a creamier result. Soaking in a stainless-steel dabba with a lid works perfectly.
